caption: A presentation slide titled "CRYOGENIC ENGINEERING" and "Tutorial – 3" is shown, focusing on a section titled "Liquid yield." The slide contains a technical diagram of a thermodynamic cycle with labeled axes, processes such as "T=const", "p=const", and "s=const", and a data table with parameters for pressure (p), temperature (T), enthalpy (h), and entropy (s) at points 1 and 2. The formula $y = \frac{h_i - h_s}{h_i - h_f}$ is displayed on the left. Across the sequence, a handwritten calculation is added in orange ink beneath the table. In the first frame, only the formula $y = \frac{h_i - h_s}{h_i - h_f}$ is written. By the second frame, the calculation $\frac{349-315}{349-60} = \frac{34}{289}$ is added. In the final frames, the calculation is completed with the result $= 0.1176$. The bottom of the slide credits "Prof. M D Atrey, Department of Mechanical Engineering, IIT Bombay".
